page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 8 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance features 150 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, which is bundled directly with the hardware. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate significant tradeoffs. The CPU and memory subsystem show lower-tier performance across several tests. Specifically, stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks fall into the bottom 10%, as do Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth write tests and Passmark database operations also rank in the bottom 10%. However, the CPU encryption test on Passmark achieves an average rating in the middle 50%, and Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput perform in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5ad.xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage at a lower resource tier, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU compute density. Suitable workloads include caching layers, memory-intensive database operations with moderate transaction volumes, and general-purpose applications requiring local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 2.2 GHz clock speed. It features 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data reveals performance tradeoffs, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%, though CPU encryption performance reaches the middle 50%. This instance provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads that require local NVMe storage but do not demand high-performance compute capabilities. It is best suited for caching layers, moderate database operations, and memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU demands.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs on AMD EPYC 7571 architecture
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz speed
150 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads and caching layers
